_comment: Signature library for tools/hooks/tooling_error_guard.py (PostToolUse). Each signature recognizes a KNOWN, permanently-fixable tooling/shell/environment failure. tier 'block' writes an incident marker (.bb2_tooling_incident.json) + blocks the turn until resolved; tier 'warn' only prints a stderr hint. MATCHING: a signature fires if ANY of its 'substrings' (matched literally, case-insensitive) OR 'regexes' (Python re, case-insensitive) appear in the tool output. ADDING A SIGNATURE HERE IS ITSELF A VALID PERMANENT FIX -- when a new failure mode is discovered, encode it so the next agent gets instant, specific guidance. Keep signatures HIGH-PRECISION (they fire on every Bash/PowerShell result); a false positive that blocks legitimate work is worse than a miss. See docs/tooling_incidents.md for the running ledger.

build_file._comment: Files that MUST use LF line endings (the GNU toolchain and bash break on CRLF). Used by the post-write CRLF check on Edit/Write/MultiEdit. A path is build-critical if its lowercased suffix is in 'suffixes', its basename is in 'names', or its basename matches a glob in 'name_globs'. Mirrors AGENTS.md 'File-edit conventions'.

signatures.summary: A shell script/command was parsed with Windows CRLF line endings -- bash choked on a trailing carriage return (\r).
signatures.root_cause: A .sh / .py / build file was saved with CRLF (\r\n) instead of LF. Usually a Windows-side editor or the Edit/Write tool introduced it silently. See memory rules crlf-via-edit-write and file-editing-wsl.
permanent_fix: Normalize the offending file to LF -- in WSL/Bash:  sed -i 's/\r$//' <file>   (or  perl -pi -e 's/\r//g' <file> ).
Prevent recurrence: confirm the file's pattern is covered by .gitattributes 'eol=lf', and edit build files via WSL/Bash (heredoc) rather than the Windows Edit/Write tool.
If a NEW path pattern was involved, add it to .gitattributes AND to build_file in this signatures file so the live CRLF check catches it next time.
signatures.suggested_guard: .gitattributes eol=lf entry covering the path + the post-write CRLF check in tooling_error_guard.py

signatures.summary: An Edit/Write/MultiEdit just wrote CRLF line endings into a build-critical file (LF is required).
signatures.root_cause: The Windows Edit/Write tool converts LF->CRLF silently when writing some files (memory: crlf-via-edit-write). The GNU toolchain / bash then break on the carriage returns.
permanent_fix: Normalize the file you just wrote back to LF:  python3 tools/normalize_lf.py <file>  (idempotent; preferred over inline sed because tools/hooks/shell_footgun_guard.py blocks inline `sed` through `wsl bash -c '...'`).
Re-make the affected target to confirm the build still matches (CRLF in a pipeline .txt or src/*.c silently corrupts codegen).
Prevent recurrence: ensure .gitattributes has an 'eol=lf' entry for this path; for the LF-critical pipeline .txt files, prefer editing via WSL rather than Edit/Write.
signatures.suggested_guard: tools/normalize_lf.py (canonical post-write remediation) + .gitattributes eol=lf entry covering the path

substrings: There is no distribution with the supplied name
Windows Subsystem for Linux has no installed distributions
signatures.summary: WSL is unavailable, not on PATH, or has no running distribution -- the Linux toolchain can't be reached.
signatures.root_cause: WSL isn't installed/started, the default distro is wrong/stopped, or 'wsl' isn't on PATH for this shell.
permanent_fix: Confirm the distro: in PowerShell  wsl -l -v ; start it / set default with  wsl --set-default <distro> .
Make the project's WSL entrypoint robust: tools/wsl.sh should detect a missing/stopped distro and emit ONE actionable line (not a raw failure). Add that detection if absent.
Document the one-time setup step in AGENTS.md (build commands) so a fresh machine never hits this blind.
signatures.suggested_guard: A preflight check in tools/wsl.sh that verifies 'wsl -l -q' lists a running distro before invoking, with a clear fix message

signatures.summary: A gitignored build dependency (cc1 / maspsx / .venv / disc / permuter) is missing.
signatures.root_cause: A gitignored build dependency was never installed, or the .venv isn't activated. Worktrees are no longer used, so there are no dep symlinks to go stale -- the deps live as real paths under the repo root.
permanent_fix: Re-install the gitignored deps:  bash tools/setup_wsl.sh   (builds gcc-2.7.2, links the mipsel toolchain, sets up the splat/m2c venv).
Confirm the build still works:  python3 -m engine.cli verify-oracle .
Prevent recurrence: ensure .venv, tools/gcc-2.7.2, tools/decomp-permuter, tools/m2c, and disc/ all exist as real paths under the repo root.
signatures.suggested_guard: Have tools/wsl.sh detect a missing required dep up front and emit one actionable line pointing at tools/setup_wsl.sh. The signature also carries suppress_if_paths_exist with the canonical core-tool paths so a path-bug error in a buggy script (e.g. relative path after cd) does not misfire when the deps are actually installed (added 2026-06-01 after the rtldump_b6c8_v2.sh false positive).

substrings: Too many levels of symbolic links
signatures.summary: A symlink is circular or points through a broken chain -- almost always a worktree dep-link gone wrong.
signatures.root_cause: A bootstrap symlink was created pointing at itself or at another symlink that points back (e.g. re-bootstrapping a worktree whose target moved).
permanent_fix: Remove the bad symlink and re-create the dep as a real path (re-run  bash tools/setup_wsl.sh  if needed).
Prevent recurrence: keep gitignored deps as real paths under the repo root rather than symlinks (worktrees, which needed symlinks, are no longer used).
signatures.suggested_guard: A check in tools/wsl.sh that a required dep resolves to a real path (not a dangling symlink).

signatures._regex_note: Tightened 4x (2026-06-04). v1=`\b(tool)\b: (command )?not found` false-positive'd against marker-file content, signature-comment cat-output, and any docs containing the phrase. v2=`(^|[\s:])(tool): command not found` still matched test fixtures like `text='bash: python3: command not found'`. v3=`^[^'"#`]*(tool): command not found` missed `sh: 1: python3: not found` (no path) and matched indented sample output. v4 (current) uses a single regex with: (a) `^(?=\S)` lookahead requiring line to start with non-whitespace (excludes indented `(sample output, not a real run)` fixture lines), (b) `[^'"#`]*?` lazy non-quote-non-hash-non-backtick prefix (excludes lines where the matched tool name appears AFTER a quote/comment/backtick), (c) optional `(command\s+)?` so both `bash: python3: command not found` (shell-builtin form) and `sh: 1: python3: not found` / `/path/python3: not found` (loader/shebang form) match. Verified by tmp/verify_sig.py against 4 real failures + 7 false-positive shapes.
signatures.summary: A core build tool (make / python3 / maspsx) is not on PATH for this shell.
signatures.root_cause: Wrong shell (Git Bash vs WSL), the .venv isn't activated, or the tool genuinely isn't installed in this environment.
permanent_fix: Route build/pipeline commands through WSL where the toolchain lives:  bash tools/wsl.sh '<cmd>'  (auto-detects cwd).
If python3 is missing only in .venv context, activate it (source .venv/bin/activate) or re-create the venv via  bash tools/setup_wsl.sh .
Prevent recurrence: if a command was run in the wrong shell, fix the caller (script/alias) to invoke via tools/wsl.sh, not raw 'make'/'python3'.
signatures.suggested_guard: Callers that need the Linux toolchain should go through tools/wsl.sh; add a PATH check at the top of any new build script

signatures._regex_note: Same FP discipline as core-tool-not-found v4 (2026-06-04): both regexes anchor at line start with `^(?=\S)` and forbid quote/hash/backtick characters in the prefix. This prevents matches against reproduced/quoted text inside test fixtures, signature comments, and incident markers.
signatures.summary: The .venv exists but its python3 symlink is missing -- pip / pyvenv-script shebangs (`#!/.venv/bin/python3`) can't exec.
signatures.root_cause: A pyvenv-style venv links .venv/bin/python3 -> /usr/bin/python3.X. If that symlink is absent (venv was created from a foreign-PATH context, or the link was cleaned), pip and any other shebanged venv script fails. PATH-fallback to /usr/bin/python3 still works for explicit `python3 ...` calls, masking the breakage until something invokes a venv shebang.
permanent_fix: Restore the symlink:  ln -sf /usr/bin/python3.12 .venv/bin/python3   (also create .venv/bin/python and .venv/bin/python3.12 if missing).
Verify pip works:    source .venv/bin/activate && pip --version .

Prevent recurrence: tools/wsl.sh now auto-repairs the symlink at invocation time when .venv/bin/activate exists but .venv/bin/python3 is missing -- so the next invocation through the canonical wrapper self-heals.
signatures.suggested_guard: tools/wsl.sh checks for .venv/bin/python3 when .venv/bin/activate exists; if missing, restore via ln -sf /usr/bin/python3.X .venv/bin/python3 (silent, idempotent).
